scores.reasoning: The homepage content describes Apify as a platform for web scraping, automation, and web data extraction with actors, datasets, key-value stores, task scheduling, and webhooks, matching the recorded description.

The API v2 documentation clearly states authentication via API tokens passed as HTTP Authorization: Bearer <API_TOKEN> and also mentions an alternative apiKey query parameter (?token=<API_TOKEN>), supporting the recorded auth methods.
The cited evidence URL for credential access returns a 404 page, providing no support for the claim that API tokens can be created self-serve in the Apify Console.

The API v2 reference lists a broad set of REST endpoints covering actors, tasks, runs, users, storages, datasets, key-value stores, request queues, webhooks, and more, confirming broad REST API coverage.
Given the public REST API docs and the general availability of API tokens (implied by other evidence), the platform is buildable today with no main blockers.
